HERE FOR THE GOOD STUFF? THESE DISCOUNT CODES ARE FOR YOU

Healthy Flourless Brownies (nut-free + gluten-free)

January 28, 2020 •
  • DF
  • GF
  • P
Jump to Recipe

Healthy Flourless Brownies made with all nut-free, paleo and gluten-free ingredients. They are the most fudgey and delicious brownie recipe to bake!

THESE BROWNIES ARE LIFE CHANGING!

Apologies for the all caps. But really…these brownies are about to change your brownie eating lifestyle for the better.

The most FUDGEY brownies that are flourless, nut-free, paleo and they are SO easy to bake.

I constantly find myself craving brownies lately. All day, everyday, ya girl just wants a thick brownie that melts in my mouth when it is warmed up. You know when the chocolate is oozing out and it’s almost too fudgey to even stay together? Ya, that kind of brownie is what we are baking today.

Now don’t get me wrong, I love all my brownie recipes. Especially those chocolate chip cookie brownies. Butt these are next level.

Probably because they require basically no ingredients (but really only 6 key ingredients are needed) and because my favorite dark chocolate is involved.

Hu Kitchen Dark Chocolate Gems! Now there is a reason that they call these bad boys gems. They live up to the name too. And let me tell you – you won’t even realize they have no refined sugar in them. They are the healthiest baking and snacking chocolate you could imagine. Sweetened with coconut sugar. Made with all organic ingredients (you know the drill with my Hu fam). Plus the gems melt so perfectly in these brownies, it is basically like eating a lava cake.

Oh and don’t forget to sprinkle some on top after the brownies bake!

Key ingredients needed to bake these flourless brownies:

  1. Creamy tahini. I use tahini in these brownies to make them nut-free and because it is more of a subtle flavored nut/seed butter. Feel free to use whatever nut butter you desire!
  2. Maple syrup. For a little sweetener here in combination with coconut sugar. Sub honey if you prefer!
  3. Coconut sugar. I love baking with coconut sugar. It adds the best sweetener and best consistency to these brownies. Plus it helps keep them refined sugar-free.
  4. Pasture-raised eggs. I haven’t tested flax eggs here. If you do and it works, let me know!
  5. Cacao powder. They key ingredient for all brownies besides the extra dark chocolate of course.
  6. Hu Kitchen Dark Chocolate Gems. My obsession. My forever baking addition. These melt SO perfectly in the brownies!! Code RACHL for free shipping ya’ll!

Once you have your gems in the pantry, here are a few of my other favorites to bake with them: Almond Flour Chocolate Chip Cookies (paleo + vegan), The Best Gluten-free Chocolate Chip Monster Cookies and Healthy Gluten-free Cheesecake Brownies (grain-free)

Print

Healthy Flourless Brownies (nut-free + gluten-free)

Print Recipe

★★★★★

5 from 75 reviews

Healthy Flourless Brownies made with all gluten-free, paleo, nut-free and dairy-free ingredients. No flour needed! These are the best FUDGEY brownies ever!

  • Author: Rachel
  • Prep Time: 5 mins
  • Cook Time: 22 mins
  • Total Time: 27-30 minutes
  • Yield: 9 brownies 1x

Ingredients

Scale
  • 1 cup tahini*
  • 1/4 cup maple syrup
  • 1/2 cup coconut sugar
  • 2 pasture-raised eggs*
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1/3 cup + 2 tablespoons cacao powder
  • 1 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1/2 cup Hu Kitchen Dark Chocolate Gems (code RACHL for free shipping)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees and line an 8×8 baking dish with parchment paper and grease well
  2. Cream together the tahini, maple syrup, coconut sugar, eggs and vanilla
  3. Mix in the cacao powder and baking powder until well combined (it will be thick!)
  4. Fold in dark chocolate gems then add batter to baking dish
  5. Bake in oven for 22-25 minutes (or until toothpick comes out clean when you poke the brownies)
  6. Allow the brownies to cool for a few minutes (this is key so they set!) then slice and enjoy!

Notes

*Can sub another nut butter

*I haven’t tested flax egg

**Store leftovers in airtight container for 5 days or freezer for longer

Did you make this recipe?

Share a photo and tag us — we can't wait to see what you've made!

Healthy Thai Pineapple Rice Salad

Family Food Prep – Week 4

2-ingredient Strawberry Banana Ice Cream

    I knew these brownies were going to be delicious when the batter was almost too good to leave alone! They taste just like any decadent brownie would. Super fudgy and soft! 10/10 and I will absolutely be making these more often!!

        Amazing! I was super skeptical because there was no flour, in fact I was expecting this recipe to have almond flour in it. However these are the most moist chewy brownies ever. I would never have baked with tahini like this before. I’ve definitely learned something new here!

  3. Amanda says:
    March 2, 2020 at 11:21 am

    I had to go pretty much completely paleo about a year ago for medical reasons, and I have been searching for a recipe that reminded me of my gluten filled sugary brownies ever since. THESE ARE IT. I’ve made them a couple of times now, and they are ALWAYS hit. No one can believe they are made with tahini! (Don’t let the tahini deter you from making these) Seriously so amazingly fudgy and delicious and the perfect amount of sweetness! Thank you, Rachel!!!

    ★★★★★

      March 2, 2020 at 3:16 pm

    Will be making again. The fiance approves, and he is not a fan of most healthy treats I make. These are so good, fudgy and soft! Taste like a good old Betty Crocker box mixed brownie. Sprinkle some Maldon flaky salt and you’ll be in heaven! 🙂

    ★★★★★

  7. Oke says:
    April 4, 2020 at 5:33 pm

    These brownies are literally so good! If I didnt make them myself, you couldnt convince me they didnt have any flour in it. The texture is great and the taste is amazing. Very filling too! I used natural peanut butter instead of tahini. I added a tbsp of evaporated milk, and I used milk chocolate chips. So good!

  20. Alissa says:
    April 8, 2020 at 10:09 pm

    I was so excited to try these because of how much I love chewy fudgey brownies, and I love the idea of being able to satisfy my craving without being too unhealthy. They were DELICIOUS. Made some substitutions which allowed me to finish a bunch of almost-empty nut butters, so I did mostly tahini, some Trader Joes mixed nut butter, and a bit of peanut butter. I also didn’t have coconut sugar so I used raw turbinado sugar since it has a similar consistency. One small mistake I made was that I accidentally used corn starch instead of baking powder (whoops, both cans looked the same!), so they may not have risen as much as they would have, but still tasted incredible. Next time I plan on adding walnuts too 🙂

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      April 9, 2020 at 10:33 am

      yay!!!

      Reply

  53. Emily says:
    January 9, 2021 at 8:49 am

    If we don’t have coaco or cacao powder, any thoughts on a substitute? Would Melted chocolate chips work?

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      January 9, 2021 at 2:09 pm

      i don’t think so – melted chocolate is liquid form while cacao and cocoa powders are more a flour/powder consistency.

      Reply
  54. Madeline Armstrong says:
    January 9, 2021 at 10:49 pm

    HOT DAMN. These are likely the best brownies I’ve EVER made, just to echo basically all of the comments here! As soon as I saw the reviews I knew I needed to make these and I was NOT disappointed! I am vegan, so I subbed the 2 eggs for 2 vegan egg replacer (bob’s red mill powder, not flax) and they turned out perfect. I also added a tiny bit less coconut sugar than it calls for. I can’t wait to make these again and again, and try different things, like diff nut butters/adding nuts/swirling PB on top… yum! Thank you for this!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      January 10, 2021 at 8:20 am

      aawwww thank you!!!

  60. Sonam says:
    January 19, 2021 at 9:37 pm

    These brownies are incredible!!! I prefer them to flour brownies. They are so decadent but don’t make you feel heavy or gross after. Thank you Rachel for sharing this recipe with us!!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      January 20, 2021 at 8:03 am

      YAY!! thank you so much!!!! xx

      Reply
  61. Lindsey says:
    January 29, 2021 at 8:45 pm

    These brownies are AMAZING!!! Such a great recipe!!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
  62. Carli Pappas says:
    January 30, 2021 at 11:28 pm

    These are UNREAL! I almost couldn’t believe all the reviews saying these were better than any brownies out there but now I know they really are. Dangerously quick and easy to make. I LOVE THESE.

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      January 31, 2021 at 4:21 pm

      wwooohhooo!!!!

      Reply
  63. Christina says:
    February 2, 2021 at 7:36 pm

    Have made this recipe countless times with whatever nut butters I have on hand and is the absolute best!! Any chance you’d ever make a single serving “mug brownie” version?

    ★★★★★

    Reply
  64. Jenny says:
    February 2, 2021 at 9:36 pm

    I can not believe how good these are! The absolute best brownie. So easy and so tasty!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      February 3, 2021 at 10:45 am

      yayyy TY!!

      Reply
  65. Becky says:
    February 8, 2021 at 3:48 pm

    Hello! As I am not making these vegan will normal eggs work?! Thank you ☺️

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      February 8, 2021 at 4:38 pm

      yes please see recipe above 😉

      Reply
  66. Kelli says:
    February 9, 2021 at 5:12 pm

    These are the real deal. SOOOO fudgy and chocolatey. Add some flaky sea salt , but otherwise perfect. i even did a half batch and microwaved in 2 mug cake ramekins and they turned out fantastic as well. Thanks Rachael

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      February 9, 2021 at 7:13 pm

      wwooohhoooooo!!!

      Reply
  67. Kelly Horsley says:
    February 11, 2021 at 3:25 pm

    Do you have the macros for any of your recipes? They all look amazing, but I’m counting macros and it would help me out tremendously. Thanks!

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      February 12, 2021 at 8:08 am

      Why You Don’t See the Nutrition Breakdown On My Recipes..

      Reply
  68. Sanny Kataoka says:
    February 18, 2021 at 3:20 pm

    I made these last week and am still enjoying them straight from the freezer. They seem to be even more delicious a few days after baking! When they were fresh, the tahini flavor was strong, so my son was not a fan, but a few days later, the tahini flavor is barely there … just chewy brownie goodness!

    I love your “real food” philosophy. I have a sweet tooth and your dessert recipes make me feel like I can indulge with less guilt. 🙂 After enjoying your instagram feed for a while now, I decided it was time to order your cookbook! Can’t wait to receive it!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      February 18, 2021 at 4:16 pm

      awwww thank you so much sanny!!

      Reply
  69. K says:
    February 19, 2021 at 8:15 pm

    What are some subs for the coconut sugar? Will organic cane sugar work? Or more maple syrup?

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      February 20, 2021 at 3:19 pm

      a grainy sugar can’t be subbed for liquid sugar in baking recipes. a mix of cane and brown may work best! xx

      Reply
  70. Val says:
    March 5, 2021 at 9:47 pm

    I made these brownies exactly as is and have decided it’ll be my go-to brownie recipe from now on! They’re deliciously chocolatey and fudgey without being overly sweet! I added a bit of sea salt on top because I’m a sucker for anything sweet and salty and my fiancé and I had to stop ourselves from finishing the whole pan! Thank you for this AMAZING recipe!!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      March 6, 2021 at 11:22 am

      aawww YAY!!!

      Reply
  71. Christine says:
    March 7, 2021 at 5:41 pm

    These were delicious!! Moist and just enough sweetness. Will definitely make it again!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      March 10, 2021 at 9:20 am

      yay!! xx

      Reply
  72. Kelly says:
    March 15, 2021 at 2:26 pm

    I make this brownie recipe about twice a month. I am the ‘weird healthy eater’ of the family, and nobody ever eats my concoctions. But damn. This recipe is INSANE, my sugar addicted family goes crazy for it. The more maple syrup the fishier the consistency is. So delicious. Rachel god bless.

    ★★★★★

    Reply
  73. Michèle K. says:
    March 29, 2021 at 4:07 am

    These brownies are so good! I will be making them forever from now on. I made them when guests came over for coffee and nobody even noticed that this was a “healthier” treat. They taste better than all recipes I’ve ever tried.

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      March 30, 2021 at 8:25 am

      yay!!!

      Reply
  74. Darlene says:
    May 13, 2021 at 1:35 am

    Just made these brownies and they were so bomb!!! Probably my favorite nut free paleo version out there. Do you have the nutrition info on these brownies? Trying to log calories, protein, carb, sugar per serving. Thanks for the amazing nut free paleo recipe!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      May 13, 2021 at 8:27 am

      happy to hear! Why You Don’t See the Nutrition Breakdown On My Recipes..

      Reply
  75. Krista says:
    May 21, 2021 at 7:09 pm

    Perfection! Easy to follow, crispy top, soft inside, rich chocolate flavor!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
  76. Kaitlyn says:
    June 10, 2021 at 11:14 pm

    I just made these brownies tonight and loved the taste, but they came out very cakey and not fudgy at all. I subbed the tahini for RX vanilla almond butter but followed the recipe exactly as written, although I did use mini chocolate chips versus Hu gems since I do not have the gems. I had to take the brownies out of the oven sooner since they were already quite hard at around 16 minutes, and have continued to firm up after cooling. Have any idea where I went wrong? Thanks for the great recipe, maybe I’ll have to add more liquid next time to get the right texture.

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      June 11, 2021 at 12:37 pm

      100% the RX nut butter.. it has more eggs in it yet? that will make them cakey 🙁 i don’t recommend that nut butter ! xx

      Reply
      1. Kaitlyn says:
        June 11, 2021 at 8:58 pm

        I tried again with regular almond butter and they turned out PERFECTLY!! Also added Hu gems which came in the mail today (yes, I ordered them for these brownies and I have no regrets lol). Absolutely loved these, thanks for a great recipe!!!

        Reply
  77. Jennifer says:
    November 19, 2021 at 3:21 am

    These brownies are delicious! I only used 1/4 cup of coconut sugar because that was all I had in and I decided to skip the chocolate chips and replace with chopped walnuts. So easy to make and so delicious!

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      November 19, 2021 at 2:55 pm

      love1!!

      Reply
  78. Jessie Kester says:
    November 22, 2021 at 9:42 pm

    Unreal! These are insanely good. Cannot believe they are flourless and no white sugar! Thank you!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      November 23, 2021 at 3:37 pm

      yayyy thank you!!

      Reply
  79. Jul says:
    December 4, 2021 at 10:17 pm

    Hello can I sub maple syrup with honey

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      December 5, 2021 at 9:49 pm

      yes!

      Reply
  80. Lindsay says:
    December 14, 2021 at 12:22 pm

    These brownies were absolutely incredible!! Can’t believe they aren’t duncan hines!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      December 14, 2021 at 8:40 pm

      ahaha yay!!!

      Reply
  81. Darrah says:
    December 18, 2021 at 9:03 am

    Another amazing recipe–thank you! I have made a number of your sweet and savory recipes, and they are all excellent and come together just as you describe. I totally trust in your recipes, and it is such a relief to have found you as I was really struggling to make quality gf baked goods, which was really frustrating (and expensive). For this recipe, I used 1/2 tahini and 1/2 almond butter, and I baked them in a muffin pan because I don’t have an 8×8 pan–they are fudgy and moist and pair perfectly with a glass of (almond) milk! All of the stars!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      December 18, 2021 at 2:47 pm

      YAYYYY

      Reply
  82. Divya Marwaha says:
    December 25, 2021 at 3:32 pm

    SO scrumptious! I have hidden them in the corner of the refrigerator so no one else easts them, lol. Are there macros to this?

    ★★★★★

    Reply
  83. Patrick B says:
    January 20, 2022 at 7:52 pm

    I’ve made these twice now (1/2 tahini, 1/2 pb), and I think they’re the best brownies I’ve ever had. Unreal recipe.

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      January 22, 2022 at 10:04 am

      aawww yay!!!!!!

      Reply
  84. Mary Beth says:
    January 22, 2022 at 8:05 pm

    I made this and used flax eggs and also added a scoop of chocolate collagen! Turned out so good!!

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      January 23, 2022 at 3:52 pm

      YAY!!

      Reply
  85. Shay says:
    January 28, 2022 at 8:37 pm

    HOLY COW! Never buying boxed brownies again. These were SO delicious, and fudgey, and EASY. How many is too many for one sitting? #help

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      January 29, 2022 at 8:14 pm

      YYYASSSS!!!! x

      Reply
  86. Lynn says:
    February 4, 2022 at 4:56 pm

    Has anyone tried using carob powder instead of cacao? If I eat this at night I cant sleep. Very sensitive to caffeine!

    Reply
    1. Jean says:
      April 18, 2022 at 10:09 am

      Yes if you don’t mind/ like the taste of carob powder then it works fine! I prefer cacao over carob but I know carob is better for my body

      Reply
  87. Linda says:
    February 11, 2022 at 3:48 pm

    Hi

    I originally tried recipe with tahini, not that crazy about flavor. This time tried peanut butter. Flavor much better. Both times seemed a little dry and I took out earlier this time. Also mine are much darker and not as thick as on photo

    Reply
  88. Dawnette Johnson says:
    March 3, 2022 at 3:11 am

    Is there anything I can sub for tahini… I can not have nuts or seeds…only pumpkin seeds..would love to make these… hard to find recipes since allergic to soy, wheat, rice, and corn

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      March 3, 2022 at 2:19 pm

      yes any nut butter!!

      Reply
  89. Jean says:
    April 18, 2022 at 10:19 am

    This is a great & easy recipe! The brownies are chocolatey and thick with the right amount of sweetness. I used this recipe recently to make birthday cupcakes. So I added an extra egg to the recipe & excluded the hu gems. Once the cupcakes were cool I melted the hu gems and dipped the top of the cupcakes in it & added a strawberry on top. They looked & tasted amazing. We are paleo and my child has developed a serious nut allergy. I’m so so glad to have found this recipe since so many paleo treats have almond flour. One question- any idea how these are after being frozen?

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      April 18, 2022 at 8:14 pm

      thank you!!! x

      Reply
  90. Jo says:
    May 12, 2022 at 3:54 am

    These are delicious and School friendly!! Thanks Rach xoxo

    ★★★★★

    Reply
    1. Rachel says:
      May 12, 2022 at 5:46 pm

      yaaay!!!!

      Reply

welcome!

hi, i'm rachel!

Welcome to my home on the internet.
I create recipes made with ‘just the good stuff’. food for everyone as I like to say. I hope you enjoy my recipes and more. I can’t wait to connect with you!
